text: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 你好,这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Beth. Neil, when you're chatting with friends, do you usually
zh: 我是Neil。我是Beth。Neil,你和朋友聊天时,

text: have the same or different opinion on things going on in the world?
zh: 对世界上发生的事情,你们通常观点相同还是不同?

text: Ooh, well, that's interesting. I think I probably have quite similar opinions and that's kind of why we're friends.
zh: 哦,这个问题很有意思。我觉得我大概和朋友们观点挺相似的,这也是我们能成为朋友的原因吧。

text: Ah, well, we might like to believe that we're accepting and tolerant of others'
zh: 啊,我们可能愿意相信自己对别人的信仰是包容和宽容的。

text: beliefs. But for many people, it can be difficult to communicate with people who have very different opinions to our own.
zh: 但对很多人来说,和观点截然不同的人沟通其实很难。

text: And this lack of acceptance could be increasing with our use of social media.
zh: 而这种不够包容的现象,可能因为我们使用社交媒体而加剧。

text: For example, algorithms tend to show us what we already like and agree with.
zh: 比如说,算法往往只给我们展示我们已经喜欢和认同的内容。

text: Of course, we can't always agree with everyone else. we all have personal opinions and beliefs on topics like religion and politics.
zh: 当然,我们不可能总是和所有人意见一致,每个人在宗教、政治这类话题上都有自己的看法和信仰。

text: But some worry that it's becoming more and more common to react with violence when they disagree than to solve disagreements together peacefully.
zh: 但有人担心,如今人们意见不合时,用暴力来回应的情况似乎越来越普遍,而不是心平气和地一起解决分歧。

text: So are we becoming a more divided society?
zh: 那么,我们的社会是否正变得越来越分裂?

text: Divided means in disagreement with and separated from others, to not be together.
zh: Divided(分裂的)意思是与他人意见不合、彼此疏离,无法团结在一起。

text: And how can we be more accepting and open to the opinions of others?
zh: 那我们该如何更包容、更开放地看待他人的观点呢?

text: That's what we'll be discussing in this programme and as usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 这就是本期节目要讨论的内容,和往常一样,我们也会学习一些实用的新单词和短语。

text: Remember, you can find all the vocabulary from this programme on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 记住,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到本期节目的所有词汇。

text: But first, I have a question for you, Beth. According to a recent Ipsos poll,
zh: 不过首先,我有一个问题要问Beth。根据益普索(Ipsos)最近的一项民调,

text: what percentage of the British population believes society is divided?
zh: 有多少比例的英国民众认为社会是分裂的?

text: Is it a. 20 % b. 50 % or c. 80 %?
Oh, I'm going to say 50%.
zh: 嗯,我猜是50%。

text: Well, we'll find out the answer later in the programme.
zh: 好,我们稍后会在节目中揭晓答案。

text: Paul Dolan, Professor of Behavioural Science at the London School of Economics,
zh: 伦敦政治经济学院行为科学教授保罗·多兰(Paul Dolan)

text: thinks we should learn how to control our emotions and be open to discussions when we disagree.
zh: 认为,我们应该学会控制情绪,在意见不合时保持开放的讨论态度。

text: Here he explains more about how to do this on BBC World Service programme All In The Mind.
zh: 在BBC世界服务节目《All In The Mind》中,他进一步解释了该如何做到这一点。

text: Generally, we're going to be making better decisions and more respectfully if we calm down.
zh: 总的来说,如果我们冷静下来,就能做出更好、也更有礼貌的决定。

text: It takes 15 minutes when you're proper aroused for the amygdala to calm down but within a few seconds it can start calming down right you take a deep breath and you start feeling calmer.
zh: 当情绪特别激动的时候,杏仁核需要15分钟才能完全平静下来,但其实只需几秒钟,它就会开始逐渐平静,比如你深吸一口气,就会开始感觉平静一些。

text: It's not to say that always being calm is good. Anger can be motivational and positive emotions actually can be very good for us.
zh: 这并不是说,保持冷静就一定是好事。愤怒有时也能激励人,而积极的情绪对我们其实也很有益处。

text: Generally people make better decisions and are more engaging and more open when they're happier
zh: 总体而言,人们在心情愉快时,往往能做出更好的决定,也更愿意投入交流、态度更开放。

text: but much of the time it will be just the case of calming down to try and take some of the heat out of the environment.
zh: 但大多数时候,关键还是要先冷静下来,给紧张的气氛降降温。

text: Paul Paul thinks we should take time to calm down when we feel aroused,
zh: 保罗认为,当我们感到aroused(情绪激动)时,应该先花时间冷静下来,

text: which here means to feel anger or strong disagreement.
zh: 这里的aroused指的是感到愤怒或强烈的不满。

text: He said it takes 15 minutes for the amygdala –
zh: 他说,当我们情绪激动时,杏仁核——

text: the part of the brain that experiences emotions – to calm down when we're aroused.
zh: 也就是大脑中负责产生情绪的部分——需要15分钟才能平静下来。

text: While anger can be motivational – make us more determined and enthusiastic –
zh: 虽然愤怒可以是motivational(有激励作用的)——让我们更坚定、更有干劲——

text: Paul says people can speak more openly when they're happier, when heat is taken out of the environment.
zh: 但保罗说,当人们心情更愉快、周围环境中的heat(火气)少一些时,他们会更愿意敞开心扉交流。

text: heat in this context means an intense feeling like anger.
zh: 在这里,heat指的是像愤怒这样强烈的情绪。

text: Now, one recent dating experiment looked into what people say they want in an ideal partner,
zh: 最近有一项关于约会的实验,研究了人们在交友资料中所说的理想伴侣标准。

text: according to their dating profile. Researchers found that many single people included details
zh: 研究人员发现,许多单身人士会在资料中写明,

text: on things like what they want their partner to look like, their height, the political party they vote for,
zh: 比如希望伴侣的外貌、身高,以及支持哪个政党等细节,

text: but many also say they want a partner who is open -minded and tolerant of different views.
zh: 但也有很多人表示,他们希望伴侣open-minded(思想开明)、能包容不同的观点。

text: If you are open -minded, you're willing to listen to and consider new ideas.
zh: 如果你是open-minded的,就是说你愿意倾听并考虑新的想法。

text: However, the researchers concluded that while people want a partner to be open -minded and tolerant,
zh: 不过,研究人员总结道,虽然人们希望伴侣思想开明、包容,

text: they don't want to be very open -minded themselves. So how can we be more open to getting to know people that we might disagree with?
zh: 但他们自己却不太愿意做到真正的开明。那么,我们该如何更愿意去了解那些和我们意见不同的人呢?

text: Here's Paul Dolan again, talking to presenter Claudia Hammond of BBC World Service's All In The Mind.
zh: 我们再次请来保罗·多兰,他与BBC世界服务《All In The Mind》节目主持人克劳迪娅·哈蒙德(Claudia Hammond)进行了交流。

text: So I guess one very obvious answer is to get offline.
zh: 我想,一个显而易见的答案就是走出网络世界。

text: I mean, to actually just get out more and meet people, because it's what political scientists think is the silver bullet.
zh: 我是说,真正多出去走走,多和人见面,因为这被政治学家认为是解决问题的silver bullet(灵丹妙药)。

text: The more you're around people that are different, the more you realise that actually they're not that different and you become more tolerant and open -minded.
zh: 你和不同的人相处得越多,就越会发现,其实他们和你没有那么不同,你也会因此变得更包容、更开放。

text: So being around people that are different, I think that's probably the way that we're going to tackle that problem.
zh: 所以,多接触和自己不同的人,我认为这大概就是我们解决这个问题的方式。

text: Paul says political scientists think the silver bullet is to get offline and meet
zh: 保罗说,政治学家认为的silver bullet就是走出网络、多与人见面。

text: people. A silver bullet means a simple solution to a complicated
zh: silver bullet的意思是,针对复杂问题的一个简单解决办法。

text: problem. By meeting people who have different opinions and beliefs to you, the more you'll realise they're not that different to you at all.
zh: 当你接触那些观点和信仰与你不同的人时,你会越来越发现,他们其实和你并没有那么不一样。

text: And that could make you more accepting and lead to a society that is less divided.
zh: 而这会让你变得更包容,从而促成一个不那么分裂的社会。

text: And speaking of divided, isn't it time you revealed the answer to your question, Neil?
zh: 说到分裂,Neil,是不是该公布你那个问题的答案了?

text: Yes, I asked what percentage of the British population believes society is divided,
zh: 是的,我问的是,根据益普索最近的一项民调,

text: according to a recent poll by the organisation Ipsos. I said 50%.
zh: 有多少比例的英国民众认为社会是分裂的。我猜的是50%。

text: Was that correct? It was not correct. It is in fact 80%. Wow, that's a lot!
zh: 对不对呢?并不对。正确答案其实是80%。哇,这个比例真高!

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned in this programme, starting with divided, which means in disagreement, not together.
zh: 好,我们来回顾一下本期节目学到的词汇,首先是divided,意思是意见不合、彼此不团结。

text: Aroused means feel a strong emotion, like anger. If something is motivational, it makes us determined and enthusiastic.
zh: Aroused指感到强烈情绪,比如愤怒。如果某样东西是motivational,它会让我们更坚定、更有干劲。

text: Heat means an intense feeling, like anger. If you are open -minded, you are willing to listen to and consider new ideas.
zh: Heat指强烈的情绪,比如愤怒。如果你是open-minded,你就愿意倾听并考虑新的想法。

text: And finally, a silver bullet means a simple solution to a complicated
zh: 最后,silver bullet的意思是,针对复杂问题的简单解决办法。

text: problem. Once again, our six minutes are up.
zh: 我们的六分钟又结束啦。

text: If you've enjoyed this programme, why not try practising the new vocabulary you've learnt with our worksheet?
zh: 如果你喜欢这期节目,不妨试试用我们的练习表来巩固今天学到的新词汇吧。

text: It's available to download now from our website, site, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 现在就可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上下载。

text: And you could also try out the quiz while you're there. Goodbye for now. Goodbye.
zh: 你还可以顺便做做那里的小测验。再见啦。再见。
